InventoryItem.title: InventoryItem
InventoryItem.description: Represents a distinct item stocked by the hotel, tracking its quantity and properties.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InventoryItem entity.

entityId.description: Reference to the hotel property entity. (Relationship: Entity 1:N InventoryItem)

name.description: The name of the inventory item (e.g., 'Hand Towel', 'Shampoo Bottle', 'Coffee Sachet').

description.description: A detailed description of the inventory item.

category.description: The category of the item (e.g., 'Linen', 'Toiletries', 'Cleaning Supplies', 'F&B', 'Office Supplies').

unitOfMeasure.description: The unit in which the item is measured and stocked (e.g., 'piece', 'bottle', 'packet', 'liter', 'kg').

currentStock.description: The current quantity of the item on hand in stock.

minStockLevel.description: The minimum stock level for this item, triggering a reorder alert if below.

maxStockLevel.description: The desired maximum stock level for this item.

lastPurchasePrice.description: The unit price of the item from its last recorded purchase.

supplierIds.description: References to Suppliers from whom this item can be purchased. (Relationship: Supplier N:N InventoryItem)

laundryItemId.description: Optional reference to a LaundryItem if this inventory item is a linen item. (Relationship: LaundryItem 1:N InventoryItem)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the inventory item was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the inventory item was last updated.

Supplier.title: Supplier
Supplier.description: Details of a vendor or supplier from whom the hotel procures inventory items.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Supplier entity.

entityId.description: Reference to the hotel property entity. (Relationship: Entity 1:N Supplier)

name.description: The official name of the supplier company.

contactPerson.description: The primary contact person at the supplier.

phone.description: The contact phone number for the supplier.

email.description: The contact email address for the supplier.

address.description: The physical address of the supplier.

notes.description: Any additional notes or details about the supplier.

isActive.description: Indicates if the supplier is currently active and can be used for purchases.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the supplier record was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the supplier record was last updated.

PurchaseOrder.title: PurchaseOrder
PurchaseOrder.description: Represents an order placed with a supplier for specific inventory items.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PurchaseOrder entity.

entityId.description: Reference to the hotel property entity. (Relationship: Entity 1:N PurchaseOrder)

supplierId.description: Reference to the Supplier for this purchase order. (Relationship: Supplier 1:N PurchaseOrder)

orderNumber.description: A unique, auto-generated or manual number for the purchase order.

orderDate.description: The date when the purchase order was placed.

expectedDeliveryDate.description: The expected date for the delivery of the ordered items.

status.description: The current status of the purchase order (e.g., 'draft', 'ordered', 'partially_received', 'received', 'cancelled').

totalAmount.description: The total monetary value of all items in the purchase order.

notes.description: Any additional notes or special instructions for the purchase order.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the purchase order was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the purchase order was last updated.

PurchaseOrderItem.title: PurchaseOrderItem
PurchaseOrderItem.description: Represents a single line item within a PurchaseOrder, detailing the specific inventory item ordered.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PurchaseOrderItem entity.

entityId.description: Reference to the hotel property entity. (Relationship: Entity 1:N PurchaseOrderItem)

purchaseOrderId.description: Reference to the parent PurchaseOrder. (Relationship: PurchaseOrder 1:N PurchaseOrderItem)

inventoryItemId.description: Reference to the InventoryItem being ordered. (Relationship: InventoryItem N:1 PurchaseOrderItem)

orderedQuantity.description: The quantity of the item that was ordered.

receivedQuantity.description: The quantity of the item that has been received so far for this order item.

unitPrice.description: The price per unit for this specific order item.

lineTotal.description: The total calculated amount for this line item (orderedQuantity * unitPrice).

notes.description: Any specific notes for this purchase order item.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the purchase order item was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the purchase order item was last updated.

StockTransaction.title: StockTransaction
StockTransaction.description: Records every movement or change in the quantity of an inventory item.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StockTransaction entity.

entityId.description: Reference to the hotel property entity. (Relationship: Entity 1:N StockTransaction)

inventoryItemId.description: Reference to the InventoryItem affected by this transaction. (Relationship: InventoryItem 1:N StockTransaction)

quantityChange.description: The change in quantity for the item. Positive for stock increase, negative for decrease.

currentStockSnapshot.description: The stock level of the item immediately after this transaction occurred.

transactionDate.description: The date and time when the stock transaction took place.

performedByUserId.description: Reference to the User who performed this stock transaction. (Relationship: User 1:N StockTransaction)

referenceDocumentId.description: Optional reference to a document that initiated this transaction (e.g., PurchaseOrderID, RoomID for consumption, InvoiceID).

notes.description: Any additional notes for this specific transaction.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the stock transaction record was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the stock transaction record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores details for each inventory item, including its current stock. Includes denormalized 'entityId' for authorization independence and 'laundryItemId' to link to linen items from the laundry section.

params.name: inventoryItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the inventory item.

definition.description: Contains information about vendors from whom inventory items are procured. Includes denormalized 'entityId' for authorization independence.

params.name: supplierId
params.description: The unique identifier for the supplier.

definition.description: Manages orders placed with suppliers for inventory items. Includes denormalized 'entityId' for authorization independence and 'supplierId' reference.

params.name: purchaseOrderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the purchase order.

definition.description: Represents individual line items within a purchase order. Includes denormalized 'entityId' for authorization independence, 'purchaseOrderId', and 'inventoryItemId' references.

params.name: purchaseOrderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the purchase order item.

definition.description: Records every movement or change in the quantity of an inventory item. Includes denormalized 'entityId' for authorization independence, 'inventoryItemId', and 'performedByUserId' references.

params.name: stockTransactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the stock transaction.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for the inventory module is designed to be secure, scalable, and highly debuggable, primarily by enforcing **Authorization Independence** and supporting **Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs)**. Each hotel property (referred to as an 'entity') is assigned a unique `entityId`, which serves as the core authorization context for all data within its scope. This `entityId` is explicitly denormalized and included as a required field in every document across the `inventory_items`, `suppliers`, `purchase_orders`, `purchase_order_items`, and `stock_transactions` collections. This critical denormalization strategy eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls in Firebase Security Rules to establish hierarchical authorization. Instead, rules can directly evaluate `request.auth.token.entityId == resource.data.entityId`, making them atomic, efficient, and straightforward to write and debug. This approach also greatly simplifies the debugging process, as all necessary authorization information is contained within the document itself.

For **Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs)**, the consistent inclusion of `entityId` in every document allows for secure and performant list operations. Frontend queries can safely and efficiently filter data using `collection.where('entityId', '==', request.auth.token.entityId)`, ensuring that users only retrieve data belonging to their assigned hotel property. This prevents security rules from being used as filters, which is inefficient and insecure. The collections maintain a **Structural Segregation** where all documents within a collection share the same security posture – all are scoped to a specific `entityId` and are internal to the PMS, thereby avoiding the complexity of mixing public and private data within the same collection.

The inventory module effectively manages stock by storing the `currentStock` in each `inventory_item` document. The user's requirement to link linen items from laundry is addressed by the `laundryItemId` reference within `inventory_items`. Purchase operations are managed through `purchase_orders` and their associated `purchase_order_items`. When a purchase is received, `stock_transactions` are created to record the movement, and the `currentStock` of the respective `inventory_item` is updated, typically via an atomic Cloud Function to maintain data integrity. This design ensures clarity, predictability, and full compliance with the specified core design principles and mandates for a robust Firebase architecture.
